pairs.question: How many times has John Calipari been named Naismith College Coach of the Year?
pairs.answer: John Calipari has been named Naismith College Coach of the Year three times: in 1996, 2008, and 2015.

pairs.question: Can you list all the teams John Calipari has coached professionally and collegiately?
pairs.answer: John Calipari has coached the following teams: University of Massachusetts (1988–1996), New Jersey Nets (NBA, 1996–1999), University of Memphis (2000–2009), University of Kentucky (2009–2024), University of Arkansas (present), Dominican Republic national team (2011, 2012), and United States men's national under-19 basketball team (2017).

pairs.question: What notable achievement did John Calipari accomplish during the 2011–2012 season?
pairs.answer: During the 2011–2012 season, John Calipari led the University of Kentucky to a national championship in college basketball.

pairs.question: How many Final Fours did John Calipari coach Kentucky to, and in which years?
pairs.answer: John Calipari coached Kentucky to four Final Fours in the years 2011, 2012, 2014, and 2015.

pairs.question: What is John Calipari's current rank among NCAA Division I all-time winningest coaches as of March 2026?
pairs.answer: As of March 2026, John Calipari ranks 5th on the NCAA Division I all-time winningest coaches list with 900 official wins.

pairs.question: Explain John Calipari's coaching career timeline with approximate years for each position.
pairs.answer: John Calipari's coaching career timeline is as follows: University of Massachusetts (1988–1996), New Jersey Nets (1996–1999), University of Memphis (2000–2009), University of Kentucky (2009–2024), Dominican Republic national team (summers of 2011 and 2012), United States men's national under-19 basketball team (July 2017), and University of Arkansas (present).

pairs.question: Provide a Python code snippet to calculate John Calipari's total number of 20-win, 30-win, and 35-win seasons as a college coach.
pairs.answer:
python
total_20_win_seasons = 29
total_30_win_seasons = 11
total_35_win_seasons = 5

print(f"John Calipari had {total_20_win_seasons} 20-win seasons, {total_30_win_seasons} 30-win seasons, and {total_35_win_seasons} 35-win seasons as a college coach.")
